checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 balance sheet (AR as of 2013-12-31, comparative 2012-12-31)

evidence.quote: Revenues=355149917.14 (2013 annual) vs Revenues=315944268.30 (2012 annual) = +12.4% YoY

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=106741826.70 (2010-12-31) growing to Goodwill=128170170.72 (2014-12-31), a +20.1% increase over 4 years
Revenues=216659277.52 (2010 annual) growing to Revenues=407508716.37 (2014 annual), a +88.1% increase over the same period

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2011/2013 cash flow and income statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?
Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly swings, e.g. NetIncomeLoss=-10187996.47 (Q2 2011) vs NetIncomeLoss=-413512.07 (Q1 2011) vs NetIncomeLoss=1189775.75 (Q3 2015)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2573926.93 (2014-12-31) and AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2920583.75 (2015-06-30), showing the allowance oscillating to zero only in intervening reported quarters

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2012-04-27, 10-K/A filed 2013-04-29, 10-K/A filed 2014-04-29, 10-K/A filed 2015-04-24 - a 10-K/A filed in nearly every fiscal year from 2008 through 2015

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data available continuously from 2008-12-31 through 2015-09-30 for Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, Assets, Liabilities, StockholdersEquity, and cash flow items

No InventoryNet or InventoryFinished/RawMaterials concept is present in the provided series, and NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities/InvestingActivities data ends at 2015-03-31, leaving Q2/Q3 2015 cash flow detail unavailable despite large asset/equity swings in that period

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Understatement or elimination of the allowance for doubtful accounts in periods with very large receivable balances (allowance reported as 0.0 in two separate quarters despite AR >$96M), which would overstate net receivables on the balance sheet and understate bad debt expense, thereby overstating net income/reducing losses in those periods.

rationale_evidence.location: 10-Q Q2 2015 balance sheet, showing a normal non-zero allowance immediately before/after the zero-reported quarters

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible acceleration of revenue recognition or extended credit terms near period-end (particularly Q4 2013), causing receivables to build up disproportionately faster than reported revenue growth, consistent with channel-stuffing or cut-off timing issues that shift revenue recognition earlier than warranted.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=111474930.42 (2013-12-31) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=82063574.94 (2013-09-30), a +35.9% quarter-over-quarter increase
SalesRevenueServicesNet=94705406.51 (Q4 2013) vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=88652530.74 (Q3 2013), only a +6.8% quarter-over-quarter increase

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Large and persistent gap between reported net losses and strongly positive, growing operating cash flow across 2011-2014 suggests heavy reliance on non-cash add-backs (D&A, stock compensation, deferred revenue growth) to generate cash while GAAP earnings remain negative; this pattern warrants scrutiny of whether expense recognition (e.g., impairments, stock comp, or deferred cost capitalization) is being used to manage reported earnings independent of underlying cash economics.

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts reported as exactly $0.0 in two non-consecutive quarters (2015-03-31 and 2015-09-30) despite receivables balances exceeding $96M-$103M, inconsistent with the ~$2.5-2.9M allowance reported in adjacent quarters

Receivables grew +31.7% YoY in FY2013 (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent) while revenue grew only +12.4% (Revenues), with a sharp +35.9% QoQ AR spike in Q4 2013 versus only +6.8% revenue growth that quarter

Persistent multi-year divergence between negative net income and strongly positive, growing operating cash flow (e.g., NetIncomeLoss=-12,260,509.06 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=61,280,260.23 in FY2014)
Recurring pattern of 10-K/A amendments filed in nearly every fiscal year from 2008-2015, plus a 10-Q/A in 2013, indicating potential recurring control or disclosure issues

Large unexplained jump in total Assets and StockholdersEquity in Q2 2015 (Assets=734,132,491.0 at 2015-06-30 vs 417,642,238.2 at 2015-03-31) coinciding with M&A-related filings (S-4, SC TO-T, 425), with no financing/investing cash flow detail provided for that period to reconcile the change
